summary: Postinstall registers a SYSTEM/root service that listens for remote commands and auto-updates from npm
details: On install, scripts/post-install.js uses node-windows to register a Windows service named 'OpenClaw Guard' (or a systemd unit on Linux) running as LocalSystem/root, with sc.exe failure recovery configured to auto-restart and sc.exe config start=auto for boot persistence. The service launches service/daemon.js → service/worker.js, which connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM endpoint (https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im, appKey 'bmdehs6pbyyks') and treats inbound IM messages as commands. service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js dispatches those messages through handleCommand/executeCommand to run command/{win,linux}/{start,stop,restart,status}.{bat,sh}, executeCommandDirect('openclaw doctor --fix'), and chatViaCLI which spawns `openclaw agent -m <attacker-supplied message> --session-id <...>` with shell:true under SYSTEM/root. service/worker.js additionally starts a DashboardReporter that periodically uploads ~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl session histories, cron jobs, approvals, projects, tasks, 7-day token-usage data, host MAC address, os.platform/arch/cpu/mem/uptime, and a host nodeId to the same server (auth token fetched from /api/claw/token/<nodeId>). On Windows the worker enumerates C:\Users to locate a real user profile and injects HOMEDRIVE/HOMEPATH/APPDATA/LOCALAPPDATA into spawned children so SYSTEM-level commands act inside individual user homes. service/updater.js polls `npm view claw-subagent-service version --json` every 6 hours and, on any newer version, runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<version>` as SYSTEM/root with no signature or hash pinning, so any future version published to this name is auto-pulled and executed with admin privileges. Package metadata is a Chinese placeholder description ('虾说智能助手') with no repository, homepage, or author email, and the C2 endpoint and appKey are hardcoded with no opt-out in metadata.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
